KFPS Friesian

KFPS Friesian Horses: The Gold Standard in Breeding Excellence and Dressage Performance

Dream Black Horse Team
March 30, 2026
2 Views
The KFPS Friesian is more than just a beautiful black horse; it is a symbol of centuries of meticulous breeding, resulting in an athletic powerhouse increasingly dominating the dressage arena.

What is the KFPS?

The Koninklijke Vereniging "Het Friesch Paarden-Stamboek" (KFPS) is the oldest studbook in the Netherlands, established in 1879. It oversees the global breeding program of Friesian horses, ensuring that only the highest quality animals contribute to the gene pool.

Breeding Standards

Only a tiny percentage of stallions are approved for breeding each year. The selection process is famously rigorous, evaluating conformation, movement, pedigree, and health. This strict adherence to standards is what makes a registered KFPS Friesian so valuable.

Dressage Excellence

Traditionally known as carriage and farm horses, modern KFPS Friesians have evolved into formidable dressage competitors. With their powerful hindquarters, elevated neck carriage, and expressive gaits, they possess a natural aptitude for collection and upper-level movements.

Frequently Asked Questions

In Conclusion

Investing in a KFPS Friesian means investing in a legacy of excellence, both in breeding and performance. Reach out to our team at Dream Black Horse to explore our carefully curated selection of KFPS Friesians.
